calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Spence for and with (petitioner) Thomas Thorpe.

The court acknowledged receipt of the documents filed yesterday but stated there had not been time for review. Mr. Spence responded to the examiner's notes regarding fees, stating that Mr. Thorpe is a very experienced professional fiduciary, they thought this would be a simple estate, they did not expect the litigation with Chase Bank and that there is nothing in the local rules or on the court's website limiting the amount of fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Clark. (Not Reported)

Petiiton For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Spence for Thomas Thorpe (Administrator).

Ms. Spence stated that they found that the Decedent had ten times the amount of property in Greece rather than Denmark, stated that the Decedent maintained residence in Greece and that the only connection to Denmark was that she was employed in Denmark, and that she should be considered a domiciliary of Greece. Mr. Spence also stated that he will be asking for additional fees and that the accounting needs to be amended. An endorsed copy of the declaration of Thomas Thorpe in support of the petition for determination is to be provided by Counsel. The petition for determination of entitlement to estate distribution was taken under submission. The accounting petition was continued to November 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Continued To Nov-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petiiton For Determination Of Entitlement To Estate Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-16-2014 Continued To Oct-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Spence for Thomas Thorpe.

The Court stated that it does not see notice to the Greek Consulate. Mr. Spence stated that the Greek government does not have an interest and that he personally served the Greek Beneficiaries and their Attorneys. Mr. Spence also addressed the issue of domicile. The Petition for Determination of Entitlement to Estate Distribution was continued to October 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if there are additional facts to support that the Decedent was domicile in Greece. The petition for final distribution was also continued to October 28, 2014 with the idea that the Court won't rule on it because Counsel plans to amend it to include a request for fees.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-11-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Spence for Thomas Thorpe. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that it seemed that the Public Adminisrtator had priority but that it may not be in the best interest of the Parties or an advantage to the Estate to have the Public Administrator serve as the roots of the Estate were in Greece. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that there were three different estates involved, that there may be tax issues both here and in Greece and that the Public Administrator would not be exerting priority in Greece. Mr. Spence stated that they were contacted by the Family in Greece, that his office asked Mr. Thorpe to assist due to his experience in international trusts and tax issues, and that this cures the jurisdictional issue. The Court granted the petition (order for the same signed in open Court). Bond in the amount of $310,000.00 is required.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
